questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Apples are Fruits.
2. All Fruits are Foods.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: Who wrote the national anthem of India?
Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
A can complete a piece of work in 13 days while B can complete the same work in 22 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 13 * 22 = 286. Rate = 13 + 22 = 35. Time = Work / Rate = 8.17 days.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
If CAT is coded as FDW, how will MAN be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 3 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 106 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 2 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.

questions.question: What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 25, 27, 29, 31, 33?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 82, 16, 36, 25
questions.explanation: 16, 25, and 36 are perfect squares. 82 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 3, 9, 27, 81, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 3. So, 81 * 3 = 243.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 3500 at an interest rate of 8% per annum over 5 years?
